title: Hire through the Rural and Francophone Community Immigration Pilots: Submit a recommendation to your community - Canada.ca
content:
## Submit a recommendation to your community
Once you’ve offered a job to a candidate, you need to complete a recommendation application and send it to the community.
The recommendation application requires you to
- show that you haven’t been able to fill the job with someone locally
- show that the job offer meets the pilot requirements
- confirm that the candidate meets all of the requirements for the pilot
- commit to connecting your candidate with immigrant settlement services in your community
You don’t need a labour market impact assessment .

## Submit the application
Contact your community directly to learn how to submit your application.

### Rural participating communities
Pictou County’s website (opens in a new tab)
North Bay’s website (opens in a new tab)
Sudbury’s website (opens in a new tab)

Thunder Bay’s website (opens in a new tab)
Steinbach’s website (opens in a new tab)
Altona/Rhineland’s website (opens in a new tab)
Brandon’s website (opens in a new tab)
Moose Jaw’s website (opens in a new tab)
Claresholm’s website (opens in a new tab)

### Francophone participating communities
Acadian Peninsula’s website (available in French only) (opens in a new tab)
Sudbury’s website (opens in a new tab)
Timmins’ website (opens in a new tab)
Superior East Region’s website (opens in a new tab)
St. Pierre Jolys’ website (opens in a new tab)
Kelowna’s website (opens in a new tab)
Once you submit it, they’ll confirm that the candidate meets all of the requirements. If you have any questions, contact your community directly.

## If your community recommends the application
If your community approves, they’ll send a recommendation certificate to the candidate. Once the candidate gets their recommendation certificate, they can apply for permanent residence.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
